tastes.title: The City of Mists
tastes.description: An apprentice cartographer discovers that her hometown is vanishing from every map, one street at a time.

sections.subtitle: Visually
sections.text: Every shot is a painting. The city itself becomes a character, with breathing rooftops and alleys that shift places at night.

sections.subtitle: Story
sections.text: A classic but sincere coming-of-age tale. ||The mist is actually the memory of the vanished inhabitants, which recontextualizes the whole first act.||
Three seasons in and the mythology still holds. The slow pace will not please everyone, but the writing of the side characters is remarkable.

sections.subtitle: Exploration
sections.text: The map unfolds like origami: every new power mentally recolors all the areas you have already visited.

sections.subtitle: Soundtrack
sections.text: Subtle pads that thicken near secrets. Clever.
A tonkotsu broth with rare depth and house-made noodles. Ten counter seats: show up before noon or settle in for a wait.
A short menu that changes weekly and rare value for money. Book ahead, the room is tiny.
A beautiful premise that runs out of breath two thirds in. An elegant pen and a few unforgettable images remain.
